response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong software engineer with staff-level experience at top-tier companies, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His primary expertise lies in TypeScript/Node.js web and cloud development; C++ is absent from his skills list and appears only in a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014 and a student thesis project. The role demands 8–12 years of deep C++ engineering with multi-threading, message brokers, secure SDL practices per IEC 62443, and industrial-automation domain knowledge — none of which are present in his background.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship discipline: consistent TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and agile practices across multiple roles
Event-driven and distributed-systems experience at scale (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic; AWS CDK and cloud-native architecture at Meta)
Staff-level engineering track record at Google and Meta, indicating strong technical leadership and cross-functional collaboration ability
concerns: C++ is absent from the skills section; only 6 months of C++ work on record (NI LabWindows, 2014–2015) — far short of the 8–12 years of hands-on C++ required
No industrial automation, IoT, MQTT, or embedded-systems experience beyond a brief stint at NI; no mention of industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
No evidence of Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) implementation or IEC 62443 industrial-cybersecurity standards knowledge
